conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for? If yes, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. My son Alex is 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

Does Alex normally live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. He lives with his mother.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. I understand that Alex lives with his mother rather than with you.

In this situation, I need to ask: Does anyone else currently claim Child Benefit for Alex (such as his mother or another person he lives with)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. This is an important detail.

Just to clarify the situation further: Even though Alex doesn't live with you, do you contribute to the cost of supporting him? For example, do you pay maintenance or contribute towards his upbringing in any way?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep. I help out with things like his clothes, school expenses, and other bits and pieces regularly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex under 16 years old, or if he's 16 or over, is he in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is 13, so he's under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you (or Alex) usually living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Can you tell me which country you're currently living in? Also, does Alex live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives in the UK with his mother. I'm living in Spain at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 13 years old (under 16) ✓
- Alex lives in the UK with his mother, not with you
- You contribute to Alex's upkeep
- You are living in Spain
- Nobody is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

Unfortunately, there are two key issues with your eligibility:

1. **Residency requirement**: To claim Child Benefit, you generally need to be living in the UK. Since you're living in Spain, you don't meet this requirement unless you fall under specific exceptions (such as certain circumstances related to working abroad for UK employers or EU regulations).

2. **The child living with you**: While it is possible to claim Child Benefit for a child who doesn't live with you if you contribute to their upkeep and no one else is claiming, this is more complex when combined with you not living in the UK yourself.

The primary eligibility would typically lie with Alex's mother, as she lives with him in the UK.

Would you like me to provide a formal assessment, or do you have any other questions about this?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK

Child is not in local authority care

Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
